title: ferray-window: functional utilities (vectorize, piecewise, apply_along_axis) are misplaced in window crate
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

ferray-window bundles functional utilities (vectorize, piecewise, apply_along_axis, apply_over_axes, sum_axis_keepdims) alongside window functions. In NumPy, these live in numpy.lib.function_base alongside the windows. But in ferray's modular architecture, these functional utilities are general-purpose and used beyond windowing. vectorize is essentially mapv, piecewise is conditional mapping, and apply_along_axis/apply_over_axes are reduction patterns. These should either: (1) move to ferray-core (since they're fundamental operations), (2) move to ferray-stats (where reductions live), or (3) stay here but rename the crate to reflect its broader scope.
